children.text: You arrive in Tsuma at the request of Magistrate Shizuko Nariko. Strange accidents plague the city—merchants fall ill from poisoned wares, dockworkers are slain in the night, and rumors whisper of a demon’s handiwork. The Crane Clan offers you honor and secrets if you can root out the source before more innocent lives are lost.

Tsuma City, a vital port of the Crane Clan, has suffered a series of subtle sabotage—poisoned market stalls, assassinations at the docks, and strange runic seals emerging in a sealed shrine. The Scorpion magistrate suspects an Oni in hiding, or worse, a rival clan’s covert agent using demonic power. The party must navigate Crane politics, survive ambushes, solve an ancestral puzzle, and finally confront the mastermind: a Masked Oni Sorcerer lurking beneath the city. Success uncovers forbidden clan lore and an ancient scroll revealing a lost technique; failure allows the Oni’s influence to spread throughout Rokugan’s heart.
Meet Magistrate Shizuko Nariko to learn about the threat to Tsuma.
“The fate of Tsuma hangs in the balance.”
Investigate the Gilded Crane district to find clues of sabotage.
Defend the docks from assassins at night.
“What stalks the pier is more than mere thieves.”
Uncover the secrets of the hidden shrine beneath Tsuma.
“Ancient wards guard more than the dead.”
Confront the mastermind orchestrating the chaos.
Magistrate Shizuko Nariko greets you in the polished halls of the Tsuma Council House. Silk banners flutter overhead, but the magistrate’s furrowed brow betrays worry.
Understand the nature of the threat and accept the mission.
Engage with Shizuko to learn the known incidents.
DC 14 to detect hidden fears or unspoken details.
Medium Humanoid (human), Lawful neutral • Magistrate of Tsuma
A poised woman in white-robes, bearing a jade fan.
A dedicated Crane diplomat torn between clan honor and city safety.
Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4 + 2) bludgeoning damage.
Born to a minor Crane noble family.
Fears a civil uprising if the culprit remains free.
Has received rumors of an Oni cult in Tsuma.
Your clan honor compels you to aid Tsuma before doom befalls the city.
At dawn in the Gilded Crane district, merchants display glittering gems and lacquerware. Suddenly, a stall erupts in coughing and shrieking—poison darts hidden in the display frames.
Locate the mechanism behind the poisoned darts.
DC 15 to spot the concealed dart launcher.
DC 13 to trace the mechanism to a rooftop panel.
A hidden crossbow mechanism fires three darts in a line whenever a gem stand is moved.
Rows of lacquered jewelry cases line the stall. A near-invisible metal rail runs along the back shelf.
You spot the tiny metal hole and pulleys under the shelf.
You disable the triggering mechanism before it can fire again.
Each triggered dart deals 2d4 piercing damage. Target must succeed on DC 13 Constitution save or take an additional 1d6 poison damage.
Moonlight glints off the tide at Tsuma docks. Suddenly, shadows detach from the barrels—three bandits and their captain rush forward.
Repel the assassins before they escape with stolen goods.
Crates provide half cover; rope coils can be cut as improvised weapons.
Medium Humanoid (any race), Neutral evil • Dockside Thug
Desperate criminals hired to terrorize the night shift.
Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d6 + 1) slashing damage.
Ranged Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, range 80/320 ft., one target. Hit: 5 (1d8 + 1) piercing damage.
Medium Humanoid (any race), Neutral evil • Pier Sergeant
Wears a chain shirt and carries a longsword.
Leader of the ambush, skilled in tactics.
The captain makes two melee attacks.
Melee Weapon Attack: +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) slashing damage.
The captain adds +3 to AC against one melee attack that would hit him.
Beneath Tsuma’s streets lies an ancient Shinto-style shrine. Cracked tōrō lanterns cast eerie shadows on faded runic inscriptions.
Solve the Five Rings puzzle to open the sealed vault.
DC 15 to interpret the order of elemental rings.
The wall bears five intertwined rings with labels: Earth, Fire, Wind, Water, Void.
DM Note: Solving reveals a hidden chamber containing an ancestral scroll.
The word “Flow” suggests Water follows Earth.
The smallest ring is Void, which closes the sequence.
Press the rings in the order: Earth → Water → Fire → Wind → Void
Listen for a soft click before moving to the next
The stone door slides open when complete
A jet of sacred wind blows out lanterns (all in the vault are blinded until they relight them).
Contains clan proverbs granting advantage on one Wisdom saving throw when invoking a proverb.
In a cavernous chamber lit by phosphorescent fungi, the Masked Oni Sorcerer stands before a black altar, chanting. Red veins mar his pale skin beneath a Noh-style mask.
Defeat the Oni Sorcerer and end the plot.
Oni Sorcerer (CR 7) with spellcasting and lair-wide aura.
3 options: Telekinetic Push, Cast Cantrip, Detect.
Large Fiend (oni), Chaotic evil • Hidden Puppeteer
Four arms beneath flowing robes, eyes burning through the mask.
A demon using Rokugani agents to sow discord. Seeks to open a permanent rift to the Shadowlands.
Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.
Any creature that starts its turn within 10 ft. must succeed on DC 15 Wisdom save or be frightened until the end of its turn.
The Oni makes two glaive attacks or casts a spell and makes one glaive attack.
Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (1d10+7) slashing damage.

The Oni hurls a mote of fire at a point it can see within 150 ft. Each creature in 20-ft radius must make DC 15 Dexterity save, taking 28 (8d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on success.
The Oni targets one creature it can see within 30 ft. and pushes it up to 10 ft.
The Oni makes a Wisdom (Perception) check.
Grants knowledge of a unique qi technique usable once.
Grants +1 bonus on Insight checks for 1 hour.
